item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Really enjoyed this. Everything is brief, only leaving in the necessary or interesting info. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thank you!! I thought I was hallucinating the spelling error. I'll edit this.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This has a lot of potential squandered by repetitive and boring test logs. Unfortunate, I really like the concept as the experience of managing multiple power strips is part of my everyday life.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I got here from the monthly contest hub. I'm not going to comment on the rest of it because I'm not sure how much I like it, but I will say that every instance of "chord" should be replaced with "cord." You could justify the current spelling with a rewrite to indicate that it's made out of biological material or make some connection to music, but I don't think the spelling was intentional. I don't feel confident messing with another author's material especially considering I'm new to the site or I'd do it myself.

item.title: Re: Junior Staff Post
item.content:encoded:
Hello!
I've changed the image to one under proper license.

License: CC BY-NC-SA 3.0
Source: ude.ffejhsaw|lpac#ude.ffejhsaw|lpac

Original site: http://capl.washjeff.edu/browseresults.php?langID=1&photoID=985&size=m

item.title: Junior Staff Post
item.content:encoded:
The image you have sourced is under the CC BY-NC-SA 3.0 license and is therefore not compliant with the SCP Wiki's CC-BY-SA 3.0 license. As such, the image has been removed. Make sure to follow this handy guide when picking any future images to make sure the license is compatible.

Stay away from NC images, and make sure you read the Image Use Guide. Wikimedia Commons is a good source for compliant images, I'd check there.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I was really hoping after seeing the picture that this SCP would be like a jumble of wires that ensnares and strangles people.

item.title: Re: Critique to your SCP.
item.content:encoded:
I anticipated that reception would be lukewarm because of the points you mentioned, but wanted to dip my toes in with something simpler first.

I have a few ideas about stuff surrounding this piece, namely the electrician and the SCP mentioned in the addendum. I'll make sure to apply stuff I've learned from writing this one and the critiques I've received here and in the draft forum.

Thank you for taking the time to write a comment. I appreciate it.

item.title: Critique to your SCP.
item.content:encoded:
Honestly, first impressions, are pretty decent!

You've stuck to the standard format, you've maintained an acceptable clinical tone throughout, and you have a decent bit of science stuff to keep me interested (i love science!)

My main issue here is that the idea isn't that interesting. This can work so much better by giving us some sort of narrative flow aside from the experiment logs. this, right now, is a magic item which does a magic thing which would've thrived in series I.

You could've added something like:

On [Insert date], SCP-XXXX was recovered in [location], alongside a journal written by [person]. The journal can be found below.

You could've pulled the "crazy science man who defies all logical laws by creating something which defies logic and does it whilst not believing in himself" trope. Let's say that this man who has created this SCP is suffering from low self-esteem and mental illness which feeds into the creation of the SCP.

by making the creator of the SCP suffer from a problem which nearly everyone has struggled with, he becomes more relatable. He's just a man who wanted to do something great with his life but didn't believe in himself and got killed by his own creation.

In your journal for this character, you could include self-deprecating insults to make him more relatable. Put emphasis on these insults and as each journal page (collapsible?) progresses, you can put emphasis on him descending into insanity as he breaks all laws of science by creating this SCP?

If you want examples of good narrative flow, you can check:

SCP-4205
SCP-087
Your Very First SCP!

Happy writing, my man! For a first SCP, this is excellent work! It's just that you need a solid idea and a good narrative flow!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I've edited the article to make it lighter and flow better. The description of the object now comes quicker to avoid boring the reader with extensive procedures before SCP-4388 is actually introduced. I've also removed impertinent details from the experiment logs, as well as redundant information.

Less is more and I'll make sure to keep that in mind if I ever write something else.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Thanks. I'm a bit confused by what you mean by "what fully happens with the power". Either it's diverted to power various things on Site-11 or it's dumped in the surrounding atmosphere, heating it up.

Do you have any tips on anything I could improve to make it clearer ?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Interesting idea, although its a little unclear on what fully happens with the power and how much it increases. +1.
